name: Granite Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Granite. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for granite surface cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Near-infrared wavelength optimized for granite mineral absorption

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning control

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning coverage

fluenceThreshold.description: Minimum fluence required for effective contaminant removal from granite

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for effective contaminant removal with minimal substrate damage

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform cleaning without excessive thermal buildup

power.description: Typical range for 1064 nm pulsed lasers in stone cleaning to avoid thermal damage to granite's low thermal conductivity (~1.5 W/m·K); starting at 100W for light contamination. Max safe below granite's damage threshold (~400°C melting point onset). Source: IPG Photonics YLR series specs and study in Optics and Lasers in Engineering (2018) on stone ablation.

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds for contamination removal on granite; slower for heavy rust/oxides (200-800 mm/s), faster for light dust (1500-3000 mm/s). Recommended 1000 mm/s balances efficiency and safety. Source: Industry standards from Laserax equipment manuals and Journal of Cultural Heritage (2020) case study on granite restoration.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for granite to achieve ablation with minimal heat-affected zone (HAZ <10 μm); picosecond (ps) or femtosecond reduces HAZ further but increases cost. Avoid >100 ns to prevent cracking. Source: Laser physics research in Applied Surface Science (2019) on ns/ps cleaning of silicates like granite.

repetition_rate.description: Typical for 1064 nm cleaning; 50 kHz recommended for granite to enable fast processing without excessive heat accumulation (granite reflectivity ~10-20% at 1064 nm aids absorption). Higher rates (>80 kHz) risk thermal buildup. Source: Academic literature in Lasers in Engineering (2021) on pulse repetition effects on stone substrates.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe contaminant removal; granite damage threshold ~6-8 J/cm². Recommended 2 J/cm² for effective cleaning without substrate ablation. Conservative due to granite's variable composition. Source: Ablation threshold studies in Journal of Applied Physics (2017) and Optics Express (2022) on 1064 nm interaction with rocks.

spot_size.description: Beam diameter for uniform coverage on granite surfaces; smaller for fine details (100-200 μm), larger for broad areas (300-500 μm). 200 μm recommended for industrial prep. Source: Equipment specifications from Coherent and Trumpf laser cleaners, aligned with industry practice for stone.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (2-5) for heavy layers on granite to ensure complete removal without overexposure. Recommended 2 passes for typical industrial use. Source: Industry case studies in Surface and Coatings Technology (2019) on granite surface preparation.

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform fluence distribution; 60% recommended for granite to avoid striping while minimizing passes. Higher (70-80%) for textured surfaces. Source: Process optimization studies in International Journal of Advanced Manufacturing Technology (2020) on laser scanning parameters for stones.
